extra.reason: Created __init__.py (PhynData dataclass, PhynConfigEntry alias, async_setup_entry/unload_entry with runtime_data + PLATFORMS forward), const.py (DOMAIN, JNAP URN constants, admin/admin creds, DEFAULT_SCAN_INTERVAL=30s), manifest.json (hassfest-ordered keys, dhcp matcher 28F537*), strings.json + translations/en.json. Verified: python3 -m py_compile on __init__.py/const.py passes; python3 -m json.tool passes on all three JSON files.

Created api.py: PhynError/PhynConnectionError, PhynDeviceInfo (defensive .get() parsing of core/GetDeviceInfo output for mac/serial/name/product/firmware), PhynDeviceState (parsed from attribute/get system/product/stats groups per device_state.local.json field names, negative flow clamped to 0.0, leak_alert via alert_notifier_fp_state_str != 'normal', freeze_risk from ml_oor_temperature_state treated as Optional[bool] since live dump showed sentinel '<unset>'), PhynLocalClient._jnap (POST http://<host>/JNAP/, Basic admin:admin, 10s timeout, raises PhynConnectionError/PhynError, unwraps optional 'output' key), get_device_info/get_state (empty-body attribute/get)/set_valve_state (Open/Close body). Verified via python3 -m py_compile (module imports aiohttp but py_compile only needs syntax, aiohttp not installed locally). SetShutoffValveState never invoked, only defined.

Created coordinator.py: PhynCoordinator(DataUpdateCoordinator[PhynDeviceState]) polling client.get_state() once per cycle at DEFAULT_SCAN_INTERVAL, wraps PhynConnectionError/PhynError as UpdateFailed; PhynEntity(CoordinatorEntity[PhynCoordinator]) base with has_entity_name=True, DeviceInfo(identifiers={(DOMAIN, serial)}, connections via dr.format_mac(mac), manufacturer Phyn, model Phyn Plus, sw_version=firmware, configuration_url from client.host), unique_id f'{serial}_{key}'. Added a public PhynLocalClient.host property (api.py) so entities can read configuration_url without reaching into a private attr. Verified with python3 -m py_compile.

Created config_flow.py: PhynLocalConfigFlow(ConfigFlow, domain=DOMAIN) with async_step_user (host form, validates via get_device_info, errors cannot_connect/unknown, sets unique_id=serial, _abort_if_unique_id_configured(updates={CONF_HOST:host}), creates entry titled device_name or 'Phyn Plus'), async_step_dhcp (probes discovery_info.ip, aborts cannot_connect on failure, same unique_id/abort-update dance, hands off to async_step_discovery_confirm), async_step_discovery_confirm (show form with name/host in description_placeholders, creates entry on confirm). DhcpServiceInfo imported from homeassistant.helpers.service_info.dhcp with fallback to homeassistant.components.dhcp. Verified with python3 -m py_compile; strings.json/translations/en.json already cover config.step.user, config.step.discovery_confirm, config.error.cannot_connect/unknown, config.abort.already_configured/cannot_connect.
Created valve.py with PhynMainValve entity, valve_open state, open/close actions, and WATER device class
Created sensor.py with 8 sensors including pressure, temperature, flow rate, consumption, flow state enum, wifi signal, valve close count, and uptime
Created binary_sensor.py with leak alert, freeze risk, and online connectivity sensors
hacs.json, LICENSE (MIT), README.md created; hacs.json JSON-validated

Added pytest suite (tests/conftest.py, test_api.py, test_config_flow.py, test_init.py) + pytest.ini; venv at .venv (Python 3.13.14, HA 2026.2.3, pytest-homeassistant-custom-component 0.13.316). 32 tests, all passing via .venv/bin/pytest tests/ -q. All device data in fixtures anonymized. No integration bugs found; no code changes to the integration.
